description: DOM clobbering — HTML elements with id/name attributes overwrite global JS variables, causing same-origin attacks via stored content (comments, profile fields, markdown).

form_clobbering.description:
Stored HTML where
or clobbers a global variable referenced by client JS. High-impact when the variable controls auth, redirect, or sink calls.

probes.description: Clobber globalConfig — reachable via document.globalConfig (named element collection). Verify in DOM after render.
Two-level clobber — document.test.action.value attacker-controlled. Common in form-action / redirect handlers.
Override DOM query API — document.querySelector becomes attacker element, breaking sanitizer logic that relies on querySelector.
Clobber document.cookie reference in client-side cookie checks. Bypasses naive 'if (!document.cookie)' patterns.
Same id repeated yields HTMLCollection — useful for clobbering arrays / iterators.
<base href> clobbering — relative URLs in subsequent <script src> or fetch() calls resolve to attacker host. Test if stored.

iframe_srcdoc_clobber.description: Sandboxed iframe srcdoc that clobbers parent globals via window.frames[N].contentWindow access patterns.

probes.description: Iframe with clobbering srcdoc. Parent's window.top read becomes the iframe's anchor.
